records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Drinking Alcohol and Reports Regarding Singing and Amusements
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5094 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"Indeed, the angels withdraw from gatherings where gambling occurs and curse the one involved in it, except in the case of betting on hoofed animals, animals with padded feet, feathered birds, and arrows. And the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) himself raced with Usamah ibn Zayd and conducted horse races."
records.arabicText: 5094 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "إِنَّ اَلْمَلاَئِكَةَ لَتَنْفِرُ عِنْدَ اَلرِّهَانِ وَ تَلْعَنُ صَاحِبَهُ مَا خَلاَ اَلْحَافِرَ وَ اَلْخُفَّ وَ اَلرِّيشَ وَ اَلنَّصْلَ وَ قَدْ سَابَقَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ أُسَامَةَ بْنَ زَيْدٍ وَ أَجْرَى اَلْخَيْلَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5094 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Indeed, the angels withdraw from gatherings where gambling occurs and curse the one involved in it, except in the case of betting on hoofed animals, animals with padded feet, feathered birds, and arrows. And the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) himself raced with Usamah ibn Zayd and conducted horse races."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Drinking Alcohol and Reports Regarding Singing and Amusements
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5095 - It has been narrated that the she-camel of the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family), was outrun in a race. So Prophet (sw), peace be upon him and his family, said:
"It (camel) transgressed and said to itself, 'Upon me is the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family.'
And it is a right upon Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, that nothing transgresses against anything except that He (swt) humiliates it.
And if a mountain were to transgress against another mountain, Allah would destroy the transgressing one among them."
records.arabicText: 5095 - فَرُوِيَ: "أَنَّ نَاقَةَ اَلنَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ سُبِقَتْ فَقَالَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "إِنَّهَا بَغَتْ وَ قَالَتْ فَوْقِي رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ وَ حَقٌّ عَلَى اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَنْ لاَ يَبْغِيَ شَيْءٌ عَلَى شَيْءٍ إِلاَّ أَذَلَّهُ اَللَّهُ وَ لَوْ أَنَّ جَبَلاً بَغَى عَلَى جَبَلٍ لَهَدَّ اَللَّهُ اَلْبَاغِيَ مِنْهُمَا" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5095 - It has been narrated that the she-camel of the Prophet (peace be upon him and his family), was outrun in a race. So Prophet (sw), peace be upon him and his family, said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"It (camel) transgressed and said to itself, 'Upon me is the Messenger of Allah (swt), peace and blessings be upon him and his family.'
And it is a right upon Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, that nothing transgresses against anything except that He (swt) humiliates it.
And if a mountain were to transgress against another mountain, Allah would destroy the transgressing one among them."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Drinking Alcohol and Reports Regarding Singing and Amusements
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5096 - The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) forbade inciting animals against one another, except in the case of dogs.
records.arabicText: 5096 - : وَ نَهَى رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ عَنْ تَحْرِيشِ اَلْبَهَائِمِ مَا خَلاَ اَلْكِلاَبَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5096 -
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) forbade inciting animals against one another, except in the case of dogs.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Drinking Alcohol and Reports Regarding Singing and Amusements
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5097 - A man asked Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) about purchasing a bondwoman with a beautiful voice.
Imam (as) said: "There is no harm upon you if you buy her and she reminds you of Paradise."
records.arabicText: 5097 - : وَ سَأَلَ رَجُلٌ عَلِيَّ بْنَ اَلْحُسَيْنِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ عَنْ شِرَاءِ جَارِيَةٍ لَهَا صَوْتٌ فَقَالَ "مَا عَلَيْكَ لَوِ اِشْتَرَيْتَهَا فَذَكَّرَتْكَ اَلْجَنَّةَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad:
Hadith.5097 - A man asked Imam Ali ibn Al-Hussain (as) about purchasing a bondwoman with a beautiful voice.
Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "There is no harm upon you if you buy her and she reminds you of Paradise."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5098 - It is narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as)  that he said:
"A servant continues to steal until, when the value of what he has stolen equals the blood money for his hand, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, exposes him."
records.arabicText: 5098 - رُوِيَ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "لاَ يَزَالُ اَلْعَبْدُ يَسْرِقُ حَتَّى إِذَا اِسْتَوْفَى دِيَةَ يَدِهِ أَظْهَرَهُ اَللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5098 - It is narrated from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as)  that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "A servant continues to steal until, when the value of what he has stolen equals the blood money for his hand, Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, exposes him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5099 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that he said:
"The hand of a thief is not to be cut off during a year of drought."
records.arabicText: 5099 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "لاَ يُقْطَعُ اَلسَّارِقُ فِي عَامِ سَنَةٍ مُجْدِبَةٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5099 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), that he said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The hand of a thief is not to be cut off during a year of drought."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5100 - In the narration of Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), it is reported: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) was presented in Kufa with a man who had stolen a pigeon, but he did not cut off his hand and said: 'I do not cut off (a hand) for stealing birds.'"
records.arabicText: 5100 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ غِيَاثِ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ : "أَنَّ عَلِيّاً عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ أُتِيَ بِالْكُوفَةِ بِرَجُلٍ سَرَقَ حَمَاماً فَلَمْ يَقْطَعْهُ وَ قَالَ "لاَ أَقْطَعُ فِي اَلطَّيْرِ" ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5100 - In the narration of Ghiyath ibn Ibrahim from Abu Abdullah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), it is reported:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) was presented in Kufa with a man who had stolen a pigeon, but he did not cut off his hand and said: 'I do not cut off (a hand) for stealing birds.'"

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5101 - Sa'd ibn Tarif narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ordered the cutting off of a hand for stealing an iron helmet and for a shield that weighed thirty-eight ratl (a unit of weight)."
records.arabicText: 5101 - وَ رَوَى سَعْدُ بْنُ طَرِيفٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "قَطَعَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي بَيْضَةِ حَدِيدٍ وَ فِي جُنَّةٍ وَزْنُهَا ثَمَانِيَةٌ وَ ثَلاَثُونَ رِطْلاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5101 - Sa'd ibn Tarif narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) ordered the cutting off of a hand for stealing an iron helmet and for a shield that weighed thirty-eight ratl (a unit of weight)."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5102 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi from Abu Abdullah (as) regarding a man who went to another man and said: "So-and-so sent me to you to collect such-and-such," and the man gave it to him, believing him.
Later, he met the original person and said: "Your messenger came to me, and I sent with him such-and-such," but the man replied:
"I did not send him to you, nor did anyone bring me anything."
The supposed messenger then claimed that he had indeed been sent and had delivered the item to him.
Imam (as) said: "If proof is established against him that he was never sent, his hand is to be cut off. But if there is no proof, the man must swear by Allah (swt) that he did not send him, and the other must recover his money from the messenger."
I asked: "What if he claims that necessity compelled him to do so?"
Imam (as) replied: "His hand is to be cut off because he has stolen the man's wealth."
records.arabicText:
5102 - وَ رَوَى حَمَّادٌ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ :
فِي رَجُلٍ أَتَى رَجُلاً فَقَالَ أَرْسَلَنِي فُلاَنٌ إِلَيْكَ لِتُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِ بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا فَأَعْطَاهُ وَ صَدَّقَهُ فَلَقِيَ صَاحِبَهُ فَقَالَ لَهُ إِنَّ رَسُولَكَ أَتَانِي فَبَعَثْتُ إِلَيْكَ مَعَهُ بِكَذَا وَ كَذَا فَقَالَ مَا أَرْسَلْتُهُ إِلَيْكَ وَ لاَ أَتَانِي أَحَدٌ بِشَيْءٍ فَزَعَمَ اَلرَّسُولُ أَنَّهُ قَدْ أَرْسَلَهُ وَ قَدْ دَفَعَهُ إِلَيْهِ قَالَ
"إِنْ وَجَدَ عَلَيْهِ بَيِّنَةً أَنَّهُ لَمْ يُرْسِلْهُ قُطِعَتْ يَدُهُ وَ إِنْ لَمْ يَجِدْ عَلَيْهِ بَيِّنَةً فَيَمِينُهُ بِاللَّهِ مَا أَرْسَلَهُ وَ يَسْتَوْفِي اَلْآخَرُ مِنَ اَلرَّسُولِ اَلْمَالَ" قُلْتُ فَإِنْ زَعَمَ أَنَّهُ حَمَلَهُ عَلَى ذَلِكَ اَلْحَاجَةُ قَالَ "يُقْطَعُ لِأَنَّهُ سَرَقَ مَالَ اَلرَّجُلِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5102 - Hammad narrated from Al-Halabi from Abu Abdullah (as)
records.thaqalaynMatn:
regarding a man who went to another man and said: "So-and-so sent me to you to collect such-and-such," and the man gave it to him, believing him.
Later, he met the original person and said: "Your messenger came to me, and I sent with him such-and-such," but the man replied:
"I did not send him to you, nor did anyone bring me anything."
The supposed messenger then claimed that he had indeed been sent and had delivered the item to him.
Imam (as) said: "If proof is established against him that he was never sent, his hand is to be cut off. But if there is no proof, the man must swear by Allah (swt) that he did not send him, and the other must recover his money from the messenger."
I asked: "What if he claims that necessity compelled him to do so?"
Imam (as) replied: "His hand is to be cut off because he has stolen the man's wealth."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5103 - It is narrated from one of the two Imams (as) that Imam (as) said:
"The hand of a thief is not to be cut off until he confesses to the theft twice. If he retracts his confession, he must compensate for what he stole, but his hand will not be cut off if there are no witnesses against him."
records.arabicText: 5103 - وَ رُوِيَ عَنْ أَحَدِهِمَا عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ أَنَّهُ قَالَ: "لاَ يُقْطَعُ اَلسَّارِقُ حَتَّى يُقِرَّ بِالسَّرِقَةِ مَرَّتَيْنِ فَإِنْ رَجَعَ ضَمِنَ اَلسَّرِقَةَ وَ لَمْ يُقْطَعْ إِذَا لَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُ شُهُودٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5103 - It is narrated from one of the two Imams (as) that Imam (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The hand of a thief is not to be cut off until he confesses to the theft twice. If he retracts his confession, he must compensate for what he stole, but his hand will not be cut off if there are no witnesses against him."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5104 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said: "Every place that can be entered without permission, and a thief steals from it, there is no cutting off of the hand."
records.arabicText: 5104 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كُلُّ مَدْخَلٍ يُدْخَلُ إِلَيْهِ بِغَيْرِ إِذْنٍ فَسَرَقَ مِنْهُ اَلسَّارِقُ فَلاَ قَطْعَ عَلَيْهِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5104 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Every place that can be entered without permission, and a thief steals from it, there is no cutting off of the hand."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5105 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said: I asked Imam (as) about a child who steals.
Imam (as) said: "If he is seven years old or younger, the punishment is lifted from him.
But if he repeats the act after the age of seven, his fingertip is to be cut off or scraped until it bleeds.
If he repeats it again, a part below his fingertip is to be cut off.
If he steals again after that and has reached nine years of age, his hand is to be cut off.
None of the prescribed punishments of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, are to be neglected."
records.arabicText: 5105 - وَ رَوَى اَلْعَلاَءُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ اَلصَّبِيِّ يَسْرِقُ قَالَ "إِنْ كَانَ لَهُ سَبْعُ سِنِينَ أَوْ أَقَلُّ رُفِعَ عَنْهُ فَإِنْ عَادَ بَعْدَ اَلسَّبْعِ قُطِعَتْ بَنَانُهُ أَوْ حُكَّتْ حَتَّى تَدْمَى فَإِنْ عَادَ قُطِعَ مِنْهُ أَسْفَلُ مِنْ بَنَانِهِ فَإِنْ عَادَ بَعْدَ ذَلِكَ وَ قَدْ بَلَغَ تِسْعَ سِنِينَ قُطِعَتْ يَدُهُ وَ لاَ يُضَيَّعُ حَدٌّ مِنْ حُدُودِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5105 - Al-Alaa narrated from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a child who steals.
Imam (as) said: "If he is seven years old or younger, the punishment is lifted from him.
But if he repeats the act after the age of seven, his fingertip is to be cut off or scraped until it bleeds.
If he repeats it again, a part below his fingertip is to be cut off.
If he steals again after that and has reached nine years of age, his hand is to be cut off.
None of the prescribed punishments of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic, are to be neglected."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5106 - A man came to Commander of the Faithful (as) and confessed to theft.
Commander of the Faithful (as) asked him: "Do you recite anything from the Book of Allah (swt), the Mighty and Majestic?"
The man replied: "Yes, Surah Al-Baqarah."
Imam (as) said: "I have pardoned your hand for the sake of Surah Al-Baqarah."
Al-Ashath objected, saying: "Are you suspending one of the punishments prescribed by Allah (swt), the Exalted?"
Commander of the Faithful (as) replied: "And what do you know about this matter? When evidence is established, the Imam has no authority to pardon, but when a man confesses against himself, then it is up to the Imam - if he wishes, he may pardon, and if he wishes, he may order the cutting."
records.arabicText: 5106 - : وَ جَاءَ رَجُلٌ إِلَى أَمِيرِ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فَأَقَرَّ بِالسَّرِقَةِ فَقَالَ لَهُ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ "أَ تَقْرَأُ شَيْئاً مِنْ كِتَابِ اَللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ" قَالَ نَعَمْ سُورَةَ اَلْبَقَرَةِ فَقَالَ "قَدْ وَهَبْتُ يَدَكَ لِسُورَةِ اَلْبَقَرَةِ " فَقَالَ اَلْأَشْعَثُ أَ تُعَطِّلُ حَدّاً مِنْ حُدُودِ اَللَّهِ تَعَالَى فَقَالَ "وَ مَا يُدْرِيكَ مَا هَذَا إِذَا قَامَتِ اَلْبَيِّنَةُ فَلَيْسَ لِلْإِمَامِ أَنْ يَعْفُوَ وَ إِذَا أَقَرَّ اَلرَّجُلُ عَلَى نَفْسِهِ فَذَاكَ إِلَى اَلْإِمَامِ إِنْ شَاءَ عَفَا وَ إِنْ شَاءَ قَطَعَ".

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5107 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said: "There is no cutting (of the hand) for stealing fruit or kathar."
And kathar refers to the inner core of palm trees (Al-jummar).
records.arabicText: 5107 - وَ فِي رِوَايَةِ اَلسَّكُونِيِّ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اَللَّهِ صَلَّى اَللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَ آلِهِ : "لاَ قَطْعَ فِي ثَمَرٍ وَ لاَ كَثَرٍ وَ اَلْكَثَرُ هُوَ اَلْجُمَّارُ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5107 - In the narration of Al-Sakuni, the Messenger of Allah (swt) (peace be upon him and his family) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no cutting (of the hand) for stealing fruit or kathar."
And kathar refers to the inner core of palm trees (Al-jummar).

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5108 - Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said: "Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled in the case of a group of people who slaughtered a camel and ate it. They were investigated to determine who among them had slaughtered it, and they all testified against themselves that they had collectively slaughtered it without specifying any individual over another. So, Imam (as) ruled that their right hands be cut off."
records.arabicText: 5108 - وَ رَوَى مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ قَيْسٍ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "قَضَى أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ فِي نَفَرٍ نَحَرُوا بَعِيراً فَأَكَلُوهُ فَامْتُحِنُوا أَيُّهُمْ نَحَرَ فَشَهِدُوا عَلَى أَنْفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ نَحَرُوهُ جَمِيعاً لَمْ يَخُصُّوا أَحَداً دُونَ أَحَدٍ فَقَضَى أَنْ تُقْطَعَ أَيْمَانُهُمْ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5108 - Muhammad ibn Qays narrated from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "Commander of the Faithful (as) ruled in the case of a group of people who slaughtered a camel and ate it. They were investigated to determine who among them had slaughtered it, and they all testified against themselves that they had collectively slaughtered it without specifying any individual over another. So, Imam (as) ruled that their right hands be cut off."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5109 - Yunus narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who stole from the war spoils an amount that would normally require his hand to be cut off.
Imam (as) said: "It should be examined how much his rightful share is. If what he took was less than his portion, he is to be disciplined (ta'zir) and given the remainder of his share.
If he took exactly what was due to him, then there is nothing upon him.
But if he took more than his share equal to the value of a shield, which is a quarter of a dinar, then his hand is to be cut off."
records.arabicText: 5109 - وَ رَوَى يُونُسُ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: قُلْتُ لَهُ رَجُلٌ سَرَقَ مِنَ اَلْمَغْنَمِ اَلشَّيْءَ اَلَّذِي يَجِبُ عَلَيْهِ اَلْقَطْعُ قَالَ "يُنْظَرُ كَمِ اَلَّذِي يُصِيبُهُ فَإِنْ كَانَ اَلَّذِي أَخَذَ أَقَلَّ مِنْ نَصِيبِهِ عُزِّرَ وَ دُفِعَ إِلَيْهِ تَمَامُ مَالِهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ أَخَذَ مِثْلَ اَلَّذِي لَهُ فَلاَ شَيْءَ عَلَيْهِ وَ إِنْ كَانَ أَخَذَ فَضْلاً بِقَدْرِ ثَمَنِ مِجَنٍّ وَ هُوَ رُبُعُ دِينَارٍ قُطِعَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5109 - Yunus narrated from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who stole from the war spoils an amount that would normally require his hand to be cut off.
Imam (as) said: "It should be examined how much his rightful share is. If what he took was less than his portion, he is to be disciplined (ta'zir) and given the remainder of his share.
If he took exactly what was due to him, then there is nothing upon him.
But if he took more than his share equal to the value of a shield, which is a quarter of a dinar, then his hand is to be cut off."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5110 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who rented a donkey and went to cloth merchants, bought a garment from them, and left the donkey with them.
Imam (as) said: "The donkey should be returned to its owner, and the one who took the garment should be pursued. However, there is no cutting (of the hand) upon him because this is an act of betrayal, not theft."
records.arabicText: 5110 - وَ رَوَى مُوسَى بْنُ بَكْرٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنْ رَجُلٍ اِكْتَرَى حِمَاراً وَ أَقْبَلَ إِلَى أَصْحَابِ اَلثِّيَابِ فَابْتَاعَ مِنْهُمْ ثَوْباً وَ تَرَكَ اَلْحِمَارَ عِنْدَهُمْ قَالَ "يُرَدُّ اَلْحِمَارُ عَلَى أَصْحَابِهِ وَ يُتْبَعُ اَلَّذِي ذَهَبَ بِالثَّوْبِ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ قَطْعٌ إِنَّمَا هِيَ خِيَانَةٌ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5110 - Musa ibn Bakr narrated from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
I asked Imam (as) about a man who rented a donkey and went to cloth merchants, bought a garment from them, and left the donkey with them.
Imam (as) said: "The donkey should be returned to its owner, and the one who took the garment should be pursued. However, there is no cutting (of the hand) upon him because this is an act of betrayal, not theft."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5111 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"When a man committed theft for the first time, Commander of the Faithful (as) would cut off his right hand. If he repeated the theft, he would cut off his left foot. If he stole a third time, he would imprison him for life and provide for him from the public treasury."
records.arabicText: 5111 - وَ قَالَ اَلصَّادِقُ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ إِذَا سَرَقَ اَلرَّجُلُ أَوَّلاً قَطَعَ يَمِينَهُ فَإِنْ عَادَ قَطَعَ رِجْلَهُ اَلْيُسْرَى فَإِنْ عَادَ ثَالِثَةً خَلَّدَهُ اَلسِّجْنَ وَ أَنْفَقَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ بَيْتِ اَلْمَالِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5111 - Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "When a man committed theft for the first time, Commander of the Faithful (as) would cut off his right hand. If he repeated the theft, he would cut off his left foot. If he stole a third time, he would imprison him for life and provide for him from the public treasury."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5112 - It has been narrated: "If he steals while in prison, he is to be executed."
records.arabicText: 5112 - وَ رُوِيَ: "أَنَّهُ إِنْ سَرَقَ فِي اَلسِّجْنِ قُتِلَ ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5112 - It has been narrated:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "If he steals while in prison, he is to be executed."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5113 - Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about the minimum amount for which a thief's hand is to be cut off.
Imam (as) said: "A quarter of a dinar."
records.arabicText: 5113 - وَ سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : عَنْ أَدْنَى مَا يُقْطَعُ فِيهِ اَلسَّارِقُ قَالَ "رُبُعُ دِينَارٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5113 - Abu Abdullah (as) was asked about the minimum amount for which a thief's hand is to be cut off.
records.thaqalaynMatn: Imam (as) said: "A quarter of a dinar."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5114 - In another narration, it is reported: "One-fifth of a dinar."
records.arabicText: 5114 - وَ فِي خَبَرٍ آخَرَ: "خُمُسُ دِينَارٍ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5114 - In another narration, it is reported:
records.thaqalaynMatn: "One-fifth of a dinar."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5115 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) regarding a man who stole, and his right hand was cut off. Then he stole again, and his left foot was cut off. Then he stole a third time.
Imam (as) said: "Commander of the Faithful (as) would imprison him for life and would say, 'I feel ashamed before my Lord (azj) to leave him without a hand to clean himself with and without a foot to walk with to fulfill his needs.'"
Imam Al-Baqir (as) added: "When Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) would cut off the hand, he would cut it below the wrist joint, and when he would cut off the foot, he would cut it from the ankle."
Imam Al-Baqir (as) also stated: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) did not believe that any of the prescribed punishments should be pardoned."
records.arabicText: 5115 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ رِئَابٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : فِي رَجُلٍ سَرَقَ فَقُطِعَتْ يَدُهُ اَلْيُمْنَى ثُمَّ سَرَقَ فَقُطِعَتْ رِجْلَهُ اَلْيُسْرَى ثُمَّ سَرَقَ اَلثَّالِثَةَ قَالَ "كَانَ أَمِيرُ اَلْمُؤْمِنِينَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ يُخَلِّدُهُ فِي اَلسِّجْنِ وَ يَقُولُ "إِنِّي لَأَسْتَحْيِي مِنْ رَبِّي أَنْ أَدَعَهُ بِلاَ يَدٍ يَسْتَنْظِفُ بِهَا وَ لاَ رِجْلٍ يَمْشِي بِهَا إِلَى حَاجَتِهِ" " قَالَ "وَ كَانَ إِذَا قَطَعَ اَلْيَدَ قَطَعَهَا دُونَ اَلْمَفْصِلِ وَ إِذَا قَطَعَ اَلرِّجْلَ قَطَعَهَا مِنَ اَلْكَعْبِ " قَالَ "وَ كَانَ لاَ يَرَى أَنْ يُعْفَى، عَنْ شَيْءٍ مِنَ اَلْحُدُودِ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5115 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Ri'ab from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as)
records.thaqalaynMatn:
regarding a man who stole, and his right hand was cut off. Then he stole again, and his left foot was cut off. Then he stole a third time.
Imam (as) said: "Commander of the Faithful (as) would imprison him for life and would say, 'I feel ashamed before my Lord (azj) to leave him without a hand to clean himself with and without a foot to walk with to fulfill his needs.'"
Imam Al-Baqir (as) added: "When Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) would cut off the hand, he would cut it below the wrist joint, and when he would cut off the foot, he would cut it from the ankle."
Imam Al-Baqir (as) also stated: "Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) did not believe that any of the prescribed punishments should be pardoned."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5116 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Al-Hasan ibn Ribat from Ibn Muskan from Al-Halabi from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
"When the legal punishment is carried out on a thief, he is to be exiled to another town. If a man steals and is not caught until he steals again and is then apprehended, and evidence is brought against him, with witnesses testifying to both the first and the most recent theft, his hand is to be cut off for the first theft, but his foot is not to be cut off for the second theft.
This is because the witnesses testified to both thefts at the same time before his hand was cut off for the first theft.
However, if the witnesses had testified regarding the first theft and his hand was cut off, and then later testified about the second theft, his left foot would be cut off."
records.arabicText: 5116 - وَ رَوَى اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلِيِّ بْنِ اَلْحَسَنِ بْنِ رِبَاطٍ عَنِ اِبْنِ مُسْكَانَ عَنِ اَلْحَلَبِيِّ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: "إِذَا أُقِيمَ عَلَى اَلسَّارِقِ اَلْحَدُّ نُفِيَ إِلَى بَلْدَةٍ أُخْرَى وَ إِنْ سَرَقَ رَجُلٌ فَلَمْ يُقْدَرْ عَلَيْهِ حَتَّى سَرَقَ مَرَّةً أُخْرَى فَأُخِذَ فَجَاءَتِ اَلْبَيِّنَةُ فَشَهِدُوا عَلَيْهِ بِالسَّرِقَةِ اَلْأُولَى وَ اَلْأَخِيرَةِ فَإِنَّهُ تُقْطَعُ يَدُهُ بِالسَّرِقَةِ اَلْأُولَى وَ لاَ تُقْطَعُ رِجْلُهُ بِالسَّرِقَةِ اَلْأَخِيرَةِ لِأَنَّ اَلشُّهُودَ شَهِدُوا عَلَيْهِ جَمِيعاً فِي مَقَامٍ وَاحِدٍ بِالسَّرِقَةِ اَلْأُولَى وَ اَلْأَخِيرَةِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تُقْطَعَ يَدُهُ بِالسَّرِقَةِ اَلْأُولَى وَ لَوْ أَنَّ اَلشُّهُودَ شَهِدُوا عَلَيْهِ بِالسَّرِقَةِ اَلْأُولَى فَقُطِعَتْ يَدُهُ ثُمَّ شَهِدُوا عَلَيْهِ بَعْدُ بِالسَّرِقَةِ اَلْأَخِيرَةِ قُطِعَتْ رِجْلُهُ اَلْيُسْرَى".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5116 - Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub narrated from Ali ibn Al-Hasan ibn Ribat from Ibn Muskan from Al-Halabi from Abu Abdullah (as) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"When the legal punishment is carried out on a thief, he is to be exiled to another town. If a man steals and is not caught until he steals again and is then apprehended, and evidence is brought against him, with witnesses testifying to both the first and the most recent theft, his hand is to be cut off for the first theft, but his foot is not to be cut off for the second theft.
This is because the witnesses testified to both thefts at the same time before his hand was cut off for the first theft.
However, if the witnesses had testified regarding the first theft and his hand was cut off, and then later testified about the second theft, his left foot would be cut off."

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText:
Hadith.5117 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
"There is no cutting (of the hand) for open and public theft, which is snatching (Al-khulsah), but I would discipline him (ta'zir).
However, the one who steals and conceals what he takes is to have his hand cut off."
There is no cutting (of the hand) for one who snatches clothing, nor is there cutting for a pickpocket (ṭarrar) if he steals from the outer garment.
However, if he steals from the inner garment, then cutting is applied.
There is also no cutting for a worker (ajir) or a guest (ḍayf) because they are entrusted.
However, it has been narrated that if a guest hosts another guest who then steals, his hand is to be cut.
If a paralyzed person (ashall) steals, his right hand is to be cut off regardless of whether it is paralyzed or healthy. If he steals again, his left foot is to be cut off. If he steals a third time, he is to be imprisoned for life, provided for from the public treasury of the Muslims, and restrained from harming people.
This was narrated by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Al-Alaa from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), and also by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as).
Additionally, there is no cutting for a slave if he steals from the property of his master because it is considered the wealth of the man himself, as if a part of him has stolen from another part.
records.arabicText:
5117 - وَ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ : "لاَ قَطْعَ فِي اَلدَّغَارَةِ اَلْمُعْلَنَةِ وَ هِيَ اَلْخُلْسَةُ وَ لَكِنِّي أُعَزِّرُهُ وَ لَكِنْ يُقْطَعُ مَنْ يَأْخُذُ وَ يُخْفِي".
وَ لَيْسَ عَلَى اَلَّذِي يَسْلُبُ اَلثِّيَابَ قَطْعٌ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَى اَلطَّرَّارِ قَطْعٌ إِذَا طَرَّ مِنَ اَلْقَمِيصِ اَلْأَعْلَى فَإِنْ طَرَّ مِنَ اَلْقَمِيصِ اَلْأَسْفَلِ فَعَلَيْهِ اَلْقَطْعُ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَى اَلْأَجِيرِ وَ لاَ عَلَى اَلضَّيْفِ قَطْعٌ لِأَنَّهُمَا مُؤْتَمَنَانِ وَ قَدْ رُوِيَ أَنَّهُ إِنْ أَضَافَ اَلضَّيْفُ ضَيْفاً فَسَرَقَ قُطِعَ وَ اَلْأَشَلُّ إِذَا سَرَقَ قُطِعَتْ يَمِينُهُ عَلَى كُلِّ حَالٍ شَلاَّءَ كَانَتْ أَوْ صَحِيحَةً فَإِنْ عَادَ فَسَرَقَ قُطِعَتْ رِجْلُهُ اَلْيُسْرَى فَإِنْ عَادَ خُلِّدَ اَلسِّجْنَ وَ أُجْرِيَ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ بَيْتِ مَالِ اَلْمُسْلِمِينَ وَ كُفَّ عَنِ اَلنَّاسِ رَوَى ذَلِكَ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَلاَءٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ زُرَارَةَ عَنْ أَبِي جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ. وَ رَوَاهُ اَلْحَسَنُ بْنُ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ وَ لَيْسَ عَلَى اَلْعَبْدِ إِذَا سَرَقَ مِنْ مَالِ مَوْلاَهُ قَطْعٌ لِأَنَّهُ مَالُ اَلرَّجُلِ سَرَقَ بَعْضُهُ بَعْضاً".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5117 - Imam Ali ibn Abi Talib (as) said:
records.thaqalaynMatn:
"There is no cutting (of the hand) for open and public theft, which is snatching (Al-khulsah), but I would discipline him (ta'zir).
However, the one who steals and conceals what he takes is to have his hand cut off."
There is no cutting (of the hand) for one who snatches clothing, nor is there cutting for a pickpocket (ṭarrar) if he steals from the outer garment.
However, if he steals from the inner garment, then cutting is applied.
There is also no cutting for a worker (ajir) or a guest (ḍayf) because they are entrusted.
However, it has been narrated that if a guest hosts another guest who then steals, his hand is to be cut.
If a paralyzed person (ashall) steals, his right hand is to be cut off regardless of whether it is paralyzed or healthy. If he steals again, his left foot is to be cut off. If he steals a third time, he is to be imprisoned for life, provided for from the public treasury of the Muslims, and restrained from harming people.
This was narrated by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Al-Alaa from Muhammad ibn Muslim from Zurarah from Abu Jafar Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), and also by Al-Hasan ibn Mahbub from Abdullah ibn Sinan from Abu Abdullah (as).
Additionally, there is no cutting for a slave if he steals from the property of his master because it is considered the wealth of the man himself, as if a part of him has stolen from another part.

records.chapter: Chapter on the Legal Punishment for Theft
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.englishText: Hadith.5118 - "The grave robber (Al-nabbash), if he is known for doing that, is to have his hand cut off."
records.arabicText: 5118 - : "وَ اَلنَّبَّاشُ إِذَا كَانَ مَعْرُوفاً بِذَلِكَ قُطِعَ".
records.thaqalaynSanad: Hadith.5118 -
records.thaqalaynMatn: "The grave robber (Al-nabbash), if he is known for doing that, is to have his hand cut off."
